para.bold: Size of the Matrix form; size1*size2.
Pointer to the lesser component. 'less_+ $t \;*$ element_size' corresponds to $(0,0)$-component of $ G^<(t,tstp) $
Pointer to the Matsubara component. 'mat_+ $\tau \;*$ element_size' corresponds to $(0,0)$-component of $ G^M(\tau) $
Number of the time grids on the Matsubara axis.
Pointer to the retarded component. 'ret_+ $t \;*$ element_size' corresponds to $(0,0)$-component of $ G^R(tstp,t) $
Number of the colums in the Matrix form.
Number of the rows in the Matrix form.
Pointer to the left-mixing component. 'tv_+ $\tau \;*$ element_size' corresponds to $(0,0)$-component of $ G^\rceil(tstp,\tau) $

memberdef.argsstring: (std::complex< T > *ret, std::complex< T > *les, std::complex< T > *tv, std::complex< T > *mat)
memberdef.name: get_data

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, int ntau, int size1, int size2, int sig)
memberdef.name: herm_matrix_timestep_view

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, GG &g, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_timestep_view< T > &g, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (std::complex< T > *data, int tstp, int ntau, int size, int sig)
memberdef.name: set_to_data

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, std::complex< T > *f0, std::complex< T > *ft, T weight=1.0)
memberdef.name: left_multiply

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, std::complex< T > *f0, std::complex< T > *ft, T weight=1.0)
memberdef.name: right_multiply

briefdescription.para: Class herm_matrix_timestep_view serves for interfacing with class herm_matrix_timestep without copying the data.

detaileddescription.para:
Purpose
Occasionally you may have a pre-defined object of herm_matrix or herm_matrix_timestep that you want to use. While one option is to make a copy of the data, most commonly you probably want to re-use this memory as a libcntr type, and this class serves as an interface. Mark similar concept of a map class in Eigen. To construct this class you need: a pointers to the region of memory defining different components (ret,tv,les,mat) and usual defining constants for the herm_matrix_timestep. The contour function can be of scalar type or matrix-valued.
Square-matrix-type contour functions are fully supported, while non-square- matrix contour functions are under development. !! NOTE: MIND DANGLING POINTERS
